test.command: python3 -m py_compile src/runtime/p2p_allreduce.py src/inference/tp_engine.py tests/test_p2p_allreduce.py tests/test_tp4_correctness.py tests/bench_tp4.py
test.notes: No local test runner available (no GPU on dev machine). Tests run remotely on GPU server during implementation. Worker handoffs confirm: test_p2p_allreduce.py PASS (TP=2,3 correctness, 1.64x speedup), test_tp4_correctness.py PASS (cosine sim 0.999990-0.999995 for 10 steps), bench_tp4.py PASS (100 steps, 12.8 tok/s). Python syntax check passed for all milestone files.

typecheck.notes: No mypy/pyright configured in project. AST parse check passed for all files.

lint.notes: No flake8/pylint configured in project. Python compile check passed.

appliedUpdates.description: Added TP=4 decode performance baseline section to architecture.md with milestone 1 benchmark results: 12.8 tok/s, 23.55 ms/tok allreduce overhead (30.1%), target milestones for improvement, and references to test files.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance for milestone 2 (threaded-dispatch) workers: The key optimization is replacing hipDeviceSynchronize() with HIP events (hipEventRecord on compute stream + hipStreamWaitEvent on P2P stream) in src/runtime/p2p_allreduce.py. This is the primary bottleneck: 128 full device syncs per token. The current hipDeviceSynchronize approach is in place for correctness - removing it without proper event dependencies will cause the ~0.1 cosine sim regression.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: tp4-engine-integration worker documented this as the future optimization path. tp4-baseline-benchmark showed 30.1% allreduce overhead is primarily from synchronization rather than data transfer. The event-based approach is already documented in architecture.md.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The kernel-optimization-worker skill should note that for multi-GPU (TP) kernel development, the standard pattern is: compile HIP C++ kernels as shared library (hipcc -shared -fPIC), expose host-callable C wrappers using hipLaunchKernelGGL (NOT hipModuleLaunchKernel), and always call (void)hipGetLastError() at the start of each C wrapper. This pattern is confirmed working for gfx906 in multi-GPU contexts where hipModuleLaunchKernel fails.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: p2p-allreduce-module worker discovered and documented this pattern. It's a systemic pattern that will apply to all future multi-GPU kernels in milestones 2 and 3.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add services.yaml test command pointing to specific test files
rejectedObservations.reason: The services.yaml test command is intentionally generic (just 'python3') because tests require remote GPU server access with Docker. Adding specific test file paths would be misleading since they can't run locally. The AGENTS.md deploy/test pattern is the right place for this information.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document duplicate kernel definitions in p2p_allreduce.hip as a library entry
rejectedObservations.reason: The duplicate kernels are non-blocking dead code in a single file. This is an implementation detail, not a pattern. Documenting it in library would add noise. The scrutiny review captures it for the worker that cleans it up.
